{{wikidata link|Q11619171}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Sugawara Tenmangu Shrine |Image = [[File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), haiden.jpg|280px]]
House of worship |Location = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Nara City|Q169134}}Sugawara Higashi 1-15-1 |ISO = JP-29 |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 41|Latitude seconds = 8.76 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 46|Longitude seconds = 42.13 |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Amenohohi|Q10940685|Amaho Nichi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Nomi-Sukune-no-Mikoto|Q965742|Nomi-Sukune-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005}}Ko |Divinity = |Company rank = {{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = {{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = October {{jalink|Sports Day}} day before |Shinto ritual = |Label position = top |Map = Nara city }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), entrance.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]]'''Sugawara Tenmangu'' (Sugawara Tenmangu) is located in {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Nara City|Q169134}} Sugawara Higashi 1-chome.{{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}}. In {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}. Also known as ``Sugawara Shrine''. It is known as a shrine related to the ancient clans {{jalink|Hashi clan|Q10927758}} and {{jalink|Sugawara clan|Q1771125}}. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are enshrined. . * {{jalink|Amenohohi|Q10940685|Amenohohi no Mikoto}} (Amenohohi no Mikoto) * {{jalink|Nominosukune|Q965742|Nominosukune no Mikoto}} (Nominosukune no Mikoto) * {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005}} (SugaharaMichizaneko) {{jalink|extension (Era name)|Q1327274|Extended}} 5 years ({{jalink|927 years|Q30470}}) “{{jalink|Engishiki|Q134244 8}}'' {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}}, the enshrined deity is listed as 1 {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1982}}. According to the shrine's legend, there was originally one enshrined deity, Tenho Nichimiko, but later Nomi no Sukune and Sugawara Michizane were enshrined, making the number three. === About the enshrined deity ===The main enshrined deity, {{jalink|Amenohohi|Q10940685|Amenohohi}}, is described in ``{{jalink|Nihonshoki|Q813935}}'' as ``Kore Izumo. As you can see, he became the ancestor god of the ancient clan '''{{jalink|Haji clan|Q10927758}}'' (Hajiuji, Hajiren and later Haji Sukune). The Haji clan is the {{jalink|Tomozo|Q11381545}} clan of Hajibu, which is responsible for the production of {{jalink|haniwa|Q849376}}, {{jalink|earthenware|Q1377111}}, funerals, and {{jalink|mausoleum|Q65292090}} {{Sfn| The Haji clan (ancient clan) |2010}}, the distant ancestor of the Haji clan, {{jalink|Nomi no Sukune|Q965742}}, is also the person whose Haniwa tradition is recorded in the Nihon Shoki. This Haji clan has four limbs, which appear as "four bells," in an article dated December 30, 9th year of {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}} ({{jalink|790 years|Q31147}}) of "{{jalink|Shoku Nihongi|Q611399}}". These are {{jalink|Izumi Province|Q1047117}} Mozu, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} Sugawara, Yamato Province Akishino, {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}}{{ jalink|Shiki District|Q11491125}}, {{jalink|Tanbi District|Q11368538}} are huge tumulus groups ({{jalink|Mozu Tumulus Group|Q4250584}}, {{jalink|Saki Dunretsu Kofun {{Sfn|Hashi clan (ancient clan)|2010}}{{Sfn|Hashi (ancient clan encyclopedia)|2015}}, which corresponds to the site of the burial mound group|Q11383489|Saki kofun group}}/{{jalink|Furuichi kofun group|Q4250076}}). Of the four tribes, the Mozu, Sugawara, and Akishino tribes were formed around the end of {{jalink|8th century|Q8086}}, respectively by the Oeda clan (later {{jalink|Oe clan|Q11436863}}) and {{jalink|Oe clan|Q11436863}}. |Sugawara clan|Q1771125}} and {{jalink|Akishino clan|Q65277285}}.The place name ``Sugawara'' means the grassland and natural habitat of {{jalink|Sedge genus|Q158501|Sedge}} (sedge), and the name originally referred to the vast hilly area west of {{jalink|Heijo Palace|Q211585}} {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shinai Shrine)|1982}}. Regarding the Haji clan branch clan in the Sugawara area, the ``Suga family biographies'' also states that the Haji clan resides as ``Since then, the Dobe clan has lived in Sugawara Fushimi Village'' {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In fact, the existence of the Haniwa kiln ruins of the late Sugawara Higashi Ruins (designated historical site by Nara City) from the late {{jalink|Kofun period|Q459939}} area is known to support the existence of the Haji clan. Sugawara Higashi Ruins Haniwa Kiln Ruins] (Nara City homepage). It is also noteworthy that the area of ​​worship of Sugawara Shrine almost coincides with the distribution range of the Haji clan. In addition to Sugawara Shrine, history books also include {{jalink|Horayama Tumulus|Q11452574|Sugawara Fushimi Toryo}} ({{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}} tomb) and Sugawara Fushimi West Mausoleum ({{jalink|Emperor Anko|Q361010}} tomb).・It is known that the name of Sugawara-dera Temple (currently {{jalink|Kikoji|Q11419051}}) is derived from the place name of this area, and the name of the Sugawara clan is also said to be derived from the name of this area {{Sfn|Sugawara clan (ancient clan)|2010}}. The rituals at Sugawara Shrine are thought to have originated when the Haji clan of Sugawara (later known as the Sugawara clan) worshiped their ancestral deity, but in later generations it became the birthplace of Michizane Sugawara. Some theories arose, and it is currently worshiped as a shrine of {{jalink|Tenjin faith|Q66890725}} {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1982}}. === History === Founding date is unknown. As mentioned above, it is presumed that the Haji clan (later {{jalink|Sugawara clan|Q1771125}}), whose home base was {{jalink|honkan|Q846706}}, worshiped their ancestral deity. Later, when Sugawara-dera Temple ({{jalink|Kikoji|Q11419051}}) was built in the southwest, it became its guardian shrine, and it came to be called both Sugawara Shrine and Kikoji Tenmangu Shrine. {{jalink|extension (Era name)|Q1327274|Extended}} "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}) {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932| In the Shinmeicho}}, ``Sugawara Shrine'' is listed in {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}{{jalink|Soshita District|Q11560415}}, and it is listed as a Shikinai Shrine {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1982}}.According to the shrine's biography, the shrine was destroyed by military fire during the {{jalink|Bunki|Q1324885}} years ({{jalink|1501|Q6131}} - {{jalink|1504|Q6148}}), and also during the {{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}} years ({{jalink|1688|Q7671}} - There was also a fire in {{jalink|1704|Q6824}}). {{jalink|Kanpo|Q1145798}} years ({{jalink|1741|Q7573}} - {{jalink|1744|Q7583}}) in {{jalink|Kofukuji|Q1070863}}{{jalink|Ichijoin|Q1135237 9}}It is said that it was rebuilt by order of the Imperial Prince Miya Makoto. After {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it became independent from Kikoji Temple due to the {{jalink|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535}}. In {{jalink|Modern Shrine Ranking System|Q712534}}, it was listed as ``Sugawara Shrine'' in {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}{{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi)|1982}}. In {{jalink|2002|Q1987}} ({{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}14), it was renamed to "Sugawara Tenmangu Shrine." === Precincts === [[File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|main hall}}]] * Main shrine * Noritoden * Hall of worship * Shrine office * Storehouse * Fudezuka * Front gate * Outside the precincts ** Tenjinbori - Called the birth pond of {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005}}. === Setsusha === * Haruhiko Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{jalink|Haruhiko Watanai|Q92665997}}Okinami * Inari Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{jalink|Toyoukebime|Q2983724|Toyouke Himekami}} * Ichikishima Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishima Himejin}} File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), keidaisha-1.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), keidaisha-2.jpg|Shinuchisha === Festival === The festivals held annually at Sugawara Tenmangu Shrine are as follows. * Monthly festival (25th of every month) * Onda Festival (February 25th) * Nara Fude Festival (March equinox) * Birthday Festival, Shinto Ritual (June 25th) * Regular festival (the day before Sports Day in October) * Niinamesai (November 25th) === Cultural assets === === Nara City designated tangible cultural property === [[File:Tosō Tenjin (Nara National Museum).jpg|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Colored Tosō Tenjin Statue on Silk
(Nara City Designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Painting on silk of Tosho Tenjin statue (painting) - Made in {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}. {{jalink|Nara National Museum|Q147312}}Deposited. Designated on March 6, 2007 [http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1258080429654/index.html Colored Tosei Tenjin statue on silk] (Nara City homepage). . === Related locations === [[File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), Sugahara-in-ato.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Sugawara Tenmangu Ruins Tenjinbori}}]] * Sugawara Tenmangu Ruins Tenjinbori ({{Coord|34|41|8.16|N|135|46|47.10|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Sugawara Tenmangu Ruins Tenjinbori}}) : The site of the Sugawara clan's old mansion. In later generations, a theory arose that it was the birthplace of Michizane Sugawara, and the pond is believed to be the birthplace of Michizane. {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1982}}. === {{wikidata link|Q11619171}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Sugawara Tenmangu Shrine |Image = [[File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), haiden.jpg|280px]]
House of worship |Location = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Nara City|Q169134}}Sugawara Higashi 1-15-1 |ISO = JP-29 |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 41|Latitude seconds = 8.76 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 46|Longitude seconds = 42.13 |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Amenohohi|Q10940685|Amaho Nichi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Nomi-Sukune-no-Mikoto|Q965742|Nomi-Sukune-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005}}Ko |Divinity = |Company rank = {{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = {{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = October {{jalink|Sports Day}} day before |Shinto ritual = |Label position = top |Map = Nara city }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), entrance.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]]'''Sugawara Tenmangu'' (Sugawara Tenmangu) is located in {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Nara City|Q169134}} Sugawara Higashi 1-chome.{{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}}. In {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}. Also known as ``Sugawara Shrine''. It is known as a shrine related to the ancient clans {{jalink|Hashi clan|Q10927758}} and {{jalink|Sugawara clan|Q1771125}}. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are enshrined. . * {{jalink|Amenohohi|Q10940685|Amenohohi no Mikoto}} (Amenohohi no Mikoto) * {{jalink|Nominosukune|Q965742|Nominosukune no Mikoto}} (Nominosukune no Mikoto) * {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005}} (SugaharaMichizaneko) {{jalink|extension (Era name)|Q1327274|Extended}} 5 years ({{jalink|927 years|Q30470}}) “{{jalink|Engishiki|Q134244 8}}'' {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}}, the enshrined deity is listed as 1 {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1982}}. According to the shrine's legend, there was originally one enshrined deity, Tenho Nichimiko, but later Nomi no Sukune and Sugawara Michizane were enshrined, making the number three. === About the enshrined deity ===The main enshrined deity, {{jalink|Amenohohi|Q10940685|Amenohohi}}, is described in ``{{jalink|Nihonshoki|Q813935}}'' as ``Kore Izumo. As you can see, he became the ancestor god of the ancient clan '''{{jalink|Haji clan|Q10927758}}'' (Hajiuji, Hajiren and later Haji Sukune). The Haji clan is the {{jalink|Tomozo|Q11381545}} clan of Hajibu, which is responsible for the production of {{jalink|haniwa|Q849376}}, {{jalink|earthenware|Q1377111}}, funerals, and {{jalink|mausoleum|Q65292090}} {{Sfn| The Haji clan (ancient clan) |2010}}, the distant ancestor of the Haji clan, {{jalink|Nomi no Sukune|Q965742}}, is also the person whose Haniwa tradition is recorded in the Nihon Shoki. This Haji clan has four limbs, which appear as "four bells," in an article dated December 30, 9th year of {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}} ({{jalink|790 years|Q31147}}) of "{{jalink|Shoku Nihongi|Q611399}}". These are {{jalink|Izumi Province|Q1047117}} Mozu, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} Sugawara, Yamato Province Akishino, {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}}{{ jalink|Shiki District|Q11491125}}, {{jalink|Tanbi District|Q11368538}} are huge tumulus groups ({{jalink|Mozu Tumulus Group|Q4250584}}, {{jalink|Saki Dunretsu Kofun {{Sfn|Hashi clan (ancient clan)|2010}}{{Sfn|Hashi (ancient clan encyclopedia)|2015}}, which corresponds to the site of the burial mound group|Q11383489|Saki kofun group}}/{{jalink|Furuichi kofun group|Q4250076}}). Of the four tribes, the Mozu, Sugawara, and Akishino tribes were formed around the end of {{jalink|8th century|Q8086}}, respectively by the Oeda clan (later {{jalink|Oe clan|Q11436863}}) and {{jalink|Oe clan|Q11436863}}. |Sugawara clan|Q1771125}} and {{jalink|Akishino clan|Q65277285}}.The place name ``Sugawara'' means the grassland and natural habitat of {{jalink|Sedge genus|Q158501|Sedge}} (sedge), and the name originally referred to the vast hilly area west of {{jalink|Heijo Palace|Q211585}} {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shinai Shrine)|1982}}. Regarding the Haji clan branch clan in the Sugawara area, the ``Suga family biographies'' also states that the Haji clan resides as ``Since then, the Dobe clan has lived in Sugawara Fushimi Village'' {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In fact, the existence of the Haniwa kiln ruins of the late Sugawara Higashi Ruins (designated historical site by Nara City) from the late {{jalink|Kofun period|Q459939}} area is known to support the existence of the Haji clan. Sugawara Higashi Ruins Haniwa Kiln Ruins] (Nara City homepage). It is also noteworthy that the area of ​​worship of Sugawara Shrine almost coincides with the distribution range of the Haji clan. In addition to Sugawara Shrine, history books also include {{jalink|Horayama Tumulus|Q11452574|Sugawara Fushimi Toryo}} ({{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}} tomb) and Sugawara Fushimi West Mausoleum ({{jalink|Emperor Anko|Q361010}} tomb).・It is known that the name of Sugawara-dera Temple (currently {{jalink|Kikoji|Q11419051}}) is derived from the place name of this area, and the name of the Sugawara clan is also said to be derived from the name of this area {{Sfn|Sugawara clan (ancient clan)|2010}}. The rituals at Sugawara Shrine are thought to have originated when the Haji clan of Sugawara (later known as the Sugawara clan) worshiped their ancestral deity, but in later generations it became the birthplace of Michizane Sugawara. Some theories arose, and it is currently worshiped as a shrine of {{jalink|Tenjin faith|Q66890725}} {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1982}}. === History === Founding date is unknown. As mentioned above, it is presumed that the Haji clan (later {{jalink|Sugawara clan|Q1771125}}), whose home base was {{jalink|honkan|Q846706}}, worshiped their ancestral deity. Later, when Sugawara-dera Temple ({{jalink|Kikoji|Q11419051}}) was built in the southwest, it became its guardian shrine, and it came to be called both Sugawara Shrine and Kikoji Tenmangu Shrine. {{jalink|extension (Era name)|Q1327274|Extended}} "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}) {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932| In the Shinmeicho}}, ``Sugawara Shrine'' is listed in {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}{{jalink|Soshita District|Q11560415}}, and it is listed as a Shikinai Shrine {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1982}}.According to the shrine's biography, the shrine was destroyed by military fire during the {{jalink|Bunki|Q1324885}} years ({{jalink|1501|Q6131}} - {{jalink|1504|Q6148}}), and also during the {{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}} years ({{jalink|1688|Q7671}} - There was also a fire in {{jalink|1704|Q6824}}). {{jalink|Kanpo|Q1145798}} years ({{jalink|1741|Q7573}} - {{jalink|1744|Q7583}}) in {{jalink|Kofukuji|Q1070863}}{{jalink|Ichijoin|Q1135237 9}}It is said that it was rebuilt by order of the Imperial Prince Miya Makoto. After {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it became independent from Kikoji Temple due to the {{jalink|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535}}. In {{jalink|Modern Shrine Ranking System|Q712534}}, it was listed as ``Sugawara Shrine'' in {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}{{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi)|1982}}. In {{jalink|2002|Q1987}} ({{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}14), it was renamed to "Sugawara Tenmangu Shrine." === Precincts === [[File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|main hall}}]] * Main shrine * Noritoden * Hall of worship * Shrine office * Storehouse * Fudezuka * Front gate * Outside the precincts ** Tenjinbori - Called the birth pond of {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005}}. === Setsusha === * Haruhiko Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{jalink|Haruhiko Watanai|Q92665997}}Okinami * Inari Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{jalink|Toyoukebime|Q2983724|Toyouke Himekami}} * Ichikishima Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishima Himejin}} File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), keidaisha-1.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), keidaisha-2.jpg|Shinuchisha === Festival === The festivals held annually at Sugawara Tenmangu Shrine are as follows. * Monthly festival (25th of every month) * Onda Festival (February 25th) * Nara Fude Festival (March equinox) * Birthday Festival, Shinto Ritual (June 25th) * Regular festival (the day before Sports Day in October) * Niinamesai (November 25th) === Cultural assets === === Nara City designated tangible cultural property === [[File:Tosō Tenjin (Nara National Museum).jpg|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Colored Tosō Tenjin Statue on Silk
(Nara City Designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Painting on silk of Tosho Tenjin statue (painting) - Made in {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}. {{jalink|Nara National Museum|Q147312}}Deposited. Designated on March 6, 2007 [http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1258080429654/index.html Colored Tosei Tenjin statue on silk] (Nara City homepage). . === Related locations === [[File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), Sugahara-in-ato.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Sugawara Tenmangu Ruins Tenjinbori}}]] * Sugawara Tenmangu Ruins Tenjinbori ({{Coord|34|41|8.16|N|135|46|47.10|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Sugawara Tenmangu Ruins Tenjinbori}}) : The site of the Sugawara clan's old mansion. In later generations, a theory arose that it was the birthplace of Michizane Sugawara, and the pond is believed to be the birthplace of Michizane. {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1982}}. === {{wikidata link|Q11619171}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Sugawara Tenmangu Shrine |Image = [[File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), haiden.jpg|280px]]
House of worship |Location = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Nara City|Q169134}}Sugawara Higashi 1-15-1 |ISO = JP-29 |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 41|Latitude seconds = 8.76 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 46|Longitude seconds = 42.13 |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Amenohohi|Q10940685|Amaho Nichi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Nomi-Sukune-no-Mikoto|Q965742|Nomi-Sukune-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005}}Ko |Divinity = |Company rank = {{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = {{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = October {{jalink|Sports Day}} day before |Shinto ritual = |Label position = top |Map = Nara city }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), entrance.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]]'''Sugawara Tenmangu'' (Sugawara Tenmangu) is located in {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Nara City|Q169134}} Sugawara Higashi 1-chome.{{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}}. In {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}. Also known as ``Sugawara Shrine''. It is known as a shrine related to the ancient clans {{jalink|Hashi clan|Q10927758}} and {{jalink|Sugawara clan|Q1771125}}. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are enshrined. . * {{jalink|Amenohohi|Q10940685|Amenohohi no Mikoto}} (Amenohohi no Mikoto) * {{jalink|Nominosukune|Q965742|Nominosukune no Mikoto}} (Nominosukune no Mikoto) * {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005}} (SugaharaMichizaneko) {{jalink|extension (Era name)|Q1327274|Extended}} 5 years ({{jalink|927 years|Q30470}}) “{{jalink|Engishiki|Q134244 8}}'' {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}}, the enshrined deity is listed as 1 {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1982}}. According to the shrine's legend, there was originally one enshrined deity, Tenho Nichimiko, but later Nomi no Sukune and Sugawara Michizane were enshrined, making the number three. === About the enshrined deity ===The main enshrined deity, {{jalink|Amenohohi|Q10940685|Amenohohi}}, is described in ``{{jalink|Nihonshoki|Q813935}}'' as ``Kore Izumo. As you can see, he became the ancestor god of the ancient clan '''{{jalink|Haji clan|Q10927758}}'' (Hajiuji, Hajiren and later Haji Sukune). The Haji clan is the {{jalink|Tomozo|Q11381545}} clan of Hajibu, which is responsible for the production of {{jalink|haniwa|Q849376}}, {{jalink|earthenware|Q1377111}}, funerals, and {{jalink|mausoleum|Q65292090}} {{Sfn| The Haji clan (ancient clan) |2010}}, the distant ancestor of the Haji clan, {{jalink|Nomi no Sukune|Q965742}}, is also the person whose Haniwa tradition is recorded in the Nihon Shoki. This Haji clan has four limbs, which appear as "four bells," in an article dated December 30, 9th year of {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}} ({{jalink|790 years|Q31147}}) of "{{jalink|Shoku Nihongi|Q611399}}". These are {{jalink|Izumi Province|Q1047117}} Mozu, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} Sugawara, Yamato Province Akishino, {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}}{{ jalink|Shiki District|Q11491125}}, {{jalink|Tanbi District|Q11368538}} are huge tumulus groups ({{jalink|Mozu Tumulus Group|Q4250584}}, {{jalink|Saki Dunretsu Kofun {{Sfn|Hashi clan (ancient clan)|2010}}{{Sfn|Hashi (ancient clan encyclopedia)|2015}}, which corresponds to the site of the burial mound group|Q11383489|Saki kofun group}}/{{jalink|Furuichi kofun group|Q4250076}}). Of the four tribes, the Mozu, Sugawara, and Akishino tribes were formed around the end of {{jalink|8th century|Q8086}}, respectively by the Oeda clan (later {{jalink|Oe clan|Q11436863}}) and {{jalink|Oe clan|Q11436863}}. |Sugawara clan|Q1771125}} and {{jalink|Akishino clan|Q65277285}}.The place name ``Sugawara'' means the grassland and natural habitat of {{jalink|Sedge genus|Q158501|Sedge}} (sedge), and the name originally referred to the vast hilly area west of {{jalink|Heijo Palace|Q211585}} {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shinai Shrine)|1982}}. Regarding the Haji clan branch clan in the Sugawara area, the ``Suga family biographies'' also states that the Haji clan resides as ``Since then, the Dobe clan has lived in Sugawara Fushimi Village'' {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In fact, the existence of the Haniwa kiln ruins of the late Sugawara Higashi Ruins (designated historical site by Nara City) from the late {{jalink|Kofun period|Q459939}} area is known to support the existence of the Haji clan. Sugawara Higashi Ruins Haniwa Kiln Ruins] (Nara City homepage). It is also noteworthy that the area of ​​worship of Sugawara Shrine almost coincides with the distribution range of the Haji clan. In addition to Sugawara Shrine, history books also include {{jalink|Horayama Tumulus|Q11452574|Sugawara Fushimi Toryo}} ({{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}} tomb) and Sugawara Fushimi West Mausoleum ({{jalink|Emperor Anko|Q361010}} tomb).・It is known that the name of Sugawara-dera Temple (currently {{jalink|Kikoji|Q11419051}}) is derived from the place name of this area, and the name of the Sugawara clan is also said to be derived from the name of this area {{Sfn|Sugawara clan (ancient clan)|2010}}. The rituals at Sugawara Shrine are thought to have originated when the Haji clan of Sugawara (later known as the Sugawara clan) worshiped their ancestral deity, but in later generations it became the birthplace of Michizane Sugawara. Some theories arose, and it is currently worshiped as a shrine of {{jalink|Tenjin faith|Q66890725}} {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1982}}. === History === Founding date is unknown. As mentioned above, it is presumed that the Haji clan (later {{jalink|Sugawara clan|Q1771125}}), whose home base was {{jalink|honkan|Q846706}}, worshiped their ancestral deity. Later, when Sugawara-dera Temple ({{jalink|Kikoji|Q11419051}}) was built in the southwest, it became its guardian shrine, and it came to be called both Sugawara Shrine and Kikoji Tenmangu Shrine. {{jalink|extension (Era name)|Q1327274|Extended}} "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}) {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932| In the Shinmeicho}}, ``Sugawara Shrine'' is listed in {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}{{jalink|Soshita District|Q11560415}}, and it is listed as a Shikinai Shrine {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1982}}.According to the shrine's biography, the shrine was destroyed by military fire during the {{jalink|Bunki|Q1324885}} years ({{jalink|1501|Q6131}} - {{jalink|1504|Q6148}}), and also during the {{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}} years ({{jalink|1688|Q7671}} - There was also a fire in {{jalink|1704|Q6824}}). {{jalink|Kanpo|Q1145798}} years ({{jalink|1741|Q7573}} - {{jalink|1744|Q7583}}) in {{jalink|Kofukuji|Q1070863}}{{jalink|Ichijoin|Q1135237 9}}It is said that it was rebuilt by order of the Imperial Prince Miya Makoto. After {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it became independent from Kikoji Temple due to the {{jalink|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535}}. In {{jalink|Modern Shrine Ranking System|Q712534}}, it was listed as ``Sugawara Shrine'' in {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}{{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi)|1982}}. In {{jalink|2002|Q1987}} ({{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}14), it was renamed to "Sugawara Tenmangu Shrine." === Precincts === [[File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|main hall}}]] * Main shrine * Noritoden * Hall of worship * Shrine office * Storehouse * Fudezuka * Front gate * Outside the precincts ** Tenjinbori - Called the birth pond of {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005}}. === Setsusha === * Haruhiko Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{jalink|Haruhiko Watanai|Q92665997}}Okinami * Inari Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{jalink|Toyoukebime|Q2983724|Toyouke Himekami}} * Ichikishima Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishima Himejin}} File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), keidaisha-1.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), keidaisha-2.jpg|Shinuchisha === Festival === The festivals held annually at Sugawara Tenmangu Shrine are as follows. * Monthly festival (25th of every month) * Onda Festival (February 25th) * Nara Fude Festival (March equinox) * Birthday Festival, Shinto Ritual (June 25th) * Regular festival (the day before Sports Day in October) * Niinamesai (November 25th) === Cultural assets === === Nara City designated tangible cultural property === [[File:Tosō Tenjin (Nara National Museum).jpg|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Colored Tosō Tenjin Statue on Silk
(Nara City Designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Painting on silk of Tosho Tenjin statue (painting) - Made in {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}. {{jalink|Nara National Museum|Q147312}}Deposited. Designated on March 6, 2007 [http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1258080429654/index.html Colored Tosei Tenjin statue on silk] (Nara City homepage). . === Related locations === [[File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), Sugahara-in-ato.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Sugawara Tenmangu Ruins Tenjinbori}}]] * Sugawara Tenmangu Ruins Tenjinbori ({{Coord|34|41|8.16|N|135|46|47.10|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Sugawara Tenmangu Ruins Tenjinbori}}) : The site of the Sugawara clan's old mansion. In later generations, a theory arose that it was the birthplace of Michizane Sugawara, and the pond is believed to be the birthplace of Michizane. {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1982}}. === {{wikidata link|Q11619171}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Sugawara Tenmangu Shrine |Image = [[File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), haiden.jpg|280px]]
House of worship |Location = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Nara City|Q169134}}Sugawara Higashi 1-15-1 |ISO = JP-29 |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 41|Latitude seconds = 8.76 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 46|Longitude seconds = 42.13 |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Amenohohi|Q10940685|Amaho Nichi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Nomi-Sukune-no-Mikoto|Q965742|Nomi-Sukune-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005}}Ko |Divinity = |Company rank = {{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = {{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = October {{jalink|Sports Day}} day before |Shinto ritual = |Label position = top |Map = Nara city }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), entrance.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]]'''Sugawara Tenmangu'' (Sugawara Tenmangu) is located in {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Nara City|Q169134}} Sugawara Higashi 1-chome.{{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}}. In {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}. Also known as ``Sugawara Shrine''. It is known as a shrine related to the ancient clans {{jalink|Hashi clan|Q10927758}} and {{jalink|Sugawara clan|Q1771125}}. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are enshrined. . * {{jalink|Amenohohi|Q10940685|Amenohohi no Mikoto}} (Amenohohi no Mikoto) * {{jalink|Nominosukune|Q965742|Nominosukune no Mikoto}} (Nominosukune no Mikoto) * {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005}} (SugaharaMichizaneko) {{jalink|extension (Era name)|Q1327274|Extended}} 5 years ({{jalink|927 years|Q30470}}) “{{jalink|Engishiki|Q134244 8}}'' {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}}, the enshrined deity is listed as 1 {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1982}}. According to the shrine's legend, there was originally one enshrined deity, Tenho Nichimiko, but later Nomi no Sukune and Sugawara Michizane were enshrined, making the number three. === About the enshrined deity ===The main enshrined deity, {{jalink|Amenohohi|Q10940685|Amenohohi}}, is described in ``{{jalink|Nihonshoki|Q813935}}'' as ``Kore Izumo. As you can see, he became the ancestor god of the ancient clan '''{{jalink|Haji clan|Q10927758}}'' (Hajiuji, Hajiren and later Haji Sukune). The Haji clan is the {{jalink|Tomozo|Q11381545}} clan of Hajibu, which is responsible for the production of {{jalink|haniwa|Q849376}}, {{jalink|earthenware|Q1377111}}, funerals, and {{jalink|mausoleum|Q65292090}} {{Sfn| The Haji clan (ancient clan) |2010}}, the distant ancestor of the Haji clan, {{jalink|Nomi no Sukune|Q965742}}, is also the person whose Haniwa tradition is recorded in the Nihon Shoki. This Haji clan has four limbs, which appear as "four bells," in an article dated December 30, 9th year of {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}} ({{jalink|790 years|Q31147}}) of "{{jalink|Shoku Nihongi|Q611399}}". These are {{jalink|Izumi Province|Q1047117}} Mozu, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} Sugawara, Yamato Province Akishino, {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}}{{ jalink|Shiki District|Q11491125}}, {{jalink|Tanbi District|Q11368538}} are huge tumulus groups ({{jalink|Mozu Tumulus Group|Q4250584}}, {{jalink|Saki Dunretsu Kofun {{Sfn|Hashi clan (ancient clan)|2010}}{{Sfn|Hashi (ancient clan encyclopedia)|2015}}, which corresponds to the site of the burial mound group|Q11383489|Saki kofun group}}/{{jalink|Furuichi kofun group|Q4250076}}). Of the four tribes, the Mozu, Sugawara, and Akishino tribes were formed around the end of {{jalink|8th century|Q8086}}, respectively by the Oeda clan (later {{jalink|Oe clan|Q11436863}}) and {{jalink|Oe clan|Q11436863}}. |Sugawara clan|Q1771125}} and {{jalink|Akishino clan|Q65277285}}.The place name ``Sugawara'' means the grassland and natural habitat of {{jalink|Sedge genus|Q158501|Sedge}} (sedge), and the name originally referred to the vast hilly area west of {{jalink|Heijo Palace|Q211585}} {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shinai Shrine)|1982}}. Regarding the Haji clan branch clan in the Sugawara area, the ``Suga family biographies'' also states that the Haji clan resides as ``Since then, the Dobe clan has lived in Sugawara Fushimi Village'' {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In fact, the existence of the Haniwa kiln ruins of the late Sugawara Higashi Ruins (designated historical site by Nara City) from the late {{jalink|Kofun period|Q459939}} area is known to support the existence of the Haji clan. Sugawara Higashi Ruins Haniwa Kiln Ruins] (Nara City homepage). It is also noteworthy that the area of ​​worship of Sugawara Shrine almost coincides with the distribution range of the Haji clan. In addition to Sugawara Shrine, history books also include {{jalink|Horayama Tumulus|Q11452574|Sugawara Fushimi Toryo}} ({{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}} tomb) and Sugawara Fushimi West Mausoleum ({{jalink|Emperor Anko|Q361010}} tomb).・It is known that the name of Sugawara-dera Temple (currently {{jalink|Kikoji|Q11419051}}) is derived from the place name of this area, and the name of the Sugawara clan is also said to be derived from the name of this area {{Sfn|Sugawara clan (ancient clan)|2010}}. The rituals at Sugawara Shrine are thought to have originated when the Haji clan of Sugawara (later known as the Sugawara clan) worshiped their ancestral deity, but in later generations it became the birthplace of Michizane Sugawara. Some theories arose, and it is currently worshiped as a shrine of {{jalink|Tenjin faith|Q66890725}} {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1982}}. === History === Founding date is unknown. As mentioned above, it is presumed that the Haji clan (later {{jalink|Sugawara clan|Q1771125}}), whose home base was {{jalink|honkan|Q846706}}, worshiped their ancestral deity. Later, when Sugawara-dera Temple ({{jalink|Kikoji|Q11419051}}) was built in the southwest, it became its guardian shrine, and it came to be called both Sugawara Shrine and Kikoji Tenmangu Shrine. {{jalink|extension (Era name)|Q1327274|Extended}} "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}) {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932| In the Shinmeicho}}, ``Sugawara Shrine'' is listed in {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}{{jalink|Soshita District|Q11560415}}, and it is listed as a Shikinai Shrine {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1982}}.According to the shrine's biography, the shrine was destroyed by military fire during the {{jalink|Bunki|Q1324885}} years ({{jalink|1501|Q6131}} - {{jalink|1504|Q6148}}), and also during the {{jalink|Genroku|Q1325001}} years ({{jalink|1688|Q7671}} - There was also a fire in {{jalink|1704|Q6824}}). {{jalink|Kanpo|Q1145798}} years ({{jalink|1741|Q7573}} - {{jalink|1744|Q7583}}) in {{jalink|Kofukuji|Q1070863}}{{jalink|Ichijoin|Q1135237 9}}It is said that it was rebuilt by order of the Imperial Prince Miya Makoto. After {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it became independent from Kikoji Temple due to the {{jalink|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535}}. In {{jalink|Modern Shrine Ranking System|Q712534}}, it was listed as ``Sugawara Shrine'' in {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}{{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi)|1982}}. In {{jalink|2002|Q1987}} ({{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}14), it was renamed to "Sugawara Tenmangu Shrine." === Precincts === [[File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|main hall}}]] * Main shrine * Noritoden * Hall of worship * Shrine office * Storehouse * Fudezuka * Front gate * Outside the precincts ** Tenjinbori - Called the birth pond of {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005}}. === Setsusha === * Haruhiko Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{jalink|Haruhiko Watanai|Q92665997}}Okinami * Inari Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{jalink|Toyoukebime|Q2983724|Toyouke Himekami}} * Ichikishima Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishima Himejin}} File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), keidaisha-1.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), keidaisha-2.jpg|Shinuchisha === Festival === The festivals held annually at Sugawara Tenmangu Shrine are as follows. * Monthly festival (25th of every month) * Onda Festival (February 25th) * Nara Fude Festival (March equinox) * Birthday Festival, Shinto Ritual (June 25th) * Regular festival (the day before Sports Day in October) * Niinamesai (November 25th) === Cultural assets === === Nara City designated tangible cultural property === [[File:Tosō Tenjin (Nara National Museum).jpg|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Colored Tosō Tenjin Statue on Silk
(Nara City Designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Painting on silk of Tosho Tenjin statue (painting) - Made in {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}. {{jalink|Nara National Museum|Q147312}}Deposited. Designated on March 6, 2007 [http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1258080429654/index.html Colored Tosei Tenjin statue on silk] (Nara City homepage). . === Related locations === [[File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), Sugahara-in-ato.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Sugawara Tenmangu Ruins Tenjinbori}}]] * Sugawara Tenmangu Ruins Tenjinbori ({{Coord|34|41|8.16|N|135|46|47.10|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Sugawara Tenmangu Ruins Tenjinbori}}) : The site of the Sugawara clan's old mansion. In later generations, a theory arose that it was the birthplace of Michizane Sugawara, and the pond is believed to be the birthplace of Michizane. {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1982}}. === In {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. Also known as ``Sugawara Shrine''. It is known as a shrine related to the ancient clans {{ill|lt=Hashi clan|Q10927758|WD=Q10927758}} and {{ill|lt=Sugawara clan|Q1771125|WD=Q1771125}}. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are enshrined. . * {{ill|lt=Amenohohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Amenohohi no Mikoto}} (Amenohohi no Mikoto) * {{ill|lt=Nominosukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nominosukune no Mikoto}} (Nominosukune no Mikoto) * {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} (SugaharaMichizaneko) {{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} 5 years ({{ill|lt=927 years|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) “{{ill||lt=Engishiki|UNKNOWN}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho}}, the enshrined deity is listed as 1 {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1982}}. According to the shrine's legend, there was originally one enshrined deity, Tenho Nichimiko, but later Nomi no Sukune and Sugawara Michizane were enshrined, making the number three. === About the enshrined deity ===The main enshrined deity, {{ill|lt=Amenohohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Amenohohi}}, is described in ``{{ill|lt=Nihonshoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}'' as ``Kore Izumo. As you can see, he became the ancestor god of the ancient clan '''{{ill|lt=Haji clan|Q10927758|WD=Q10927758}}'' (Hajiuji, Hajiren and later Haji Sukune). The Haji clan is the {{ill|lt=Tomozo|Q11381545|WD=Q11381545}} clan of Hajibu, which is responsible for the production of {{ill|lt=haniwa|Q849376|WD=Q849376}}, {{ill|lt=earthenware|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111}}, funerals, and {{ill|lt=mausoleum|Q65292090|WD=Q65292090}} {{Sfn| The Haji clan (ancient clan) |2010}}, the distant ancestor of the Haji clan, {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742}}, is also the person whose Haniwa tradition is recorded in the Nihon Shoki. This Haji clan has four limbs, which appear as "four bells," in an article dated December 30, 9th year of {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} ({{ill|lt=790 years|Q31147|WD=Q31147}}) of "{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}". These are {{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q1047117|WD=Q1047117}} Mozu, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} Sugawara, Yamato Province Akishino, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}}{{ jalink|Shiki District|Q11491125}}, {{ill|lt=Tanbi District|Q11368538|WD=Q11368538}} are huge tumulus groups ({{ill|lt=Mozu Tumulus Group|Q4250584|WD=Q4250584}}, {{jalink|Saki Dunretsu Kofun {{Sfn|Hashi clan (ancient clan)|2010}}{{Sfn|Hashi (ancient clan encyclopedia)|2015}}, which corresponds to the site of the burial mound group|Q11383489|Saki kofun group}}/{{ill|lt=Furuichi kofun group|Q4250076|WD=Q4250076}}). Of the four tribes, the Mozu, Sugawara, and Akishino tribes were formed around the end of {{ill|lt=8th century|Q8086|WD=Q8086}}, respectively by the Oeda clan (later {{ill|lt=Oe clan|Q11436863|WD=Q11436863}}) and {{ill|lt=Oe clan|Q11436863|WD=Q11436863}}. |Sugawara clan|Q1771125}} and {{ill|lt=Akishino clan|Q65277285|WD=Q65277285}}.The place name ``Sugawara'' means the grassland and natural habitat of {{ill|lt=Sedge genus|Q158501|WD=Q158501|lt=Sedge}} (sedge), and the name originally referred to the vast hilly area west of {{ill|lt=Heijo Palace|Q211585|WD=Q211585}} {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shinai Shrine)|1982}}. Regarding the Haji clan branch clan in the Sugawara area, the ``Suga family biographies'' also states that the Haji clan resides as ``Since then, the Dobe clan has lived in Sugawara Fushimi Village'' {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In fact, the existence of the Haniwa kiln ruins of the late Sugawara Higashi Ruins (designated historical site by Nara City) from the late {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939}} area is known to support the existence of the Haji clan. Sugawara Higashi Ruins Haniwa Kiln Ruins] (Nara City homepage). It is also noteworthy that the area of ​​worship of Sugawara Shrine almost coincides with the distribution range of the Haji clan. In addition to Sugawara Shrine, history books also include {{ill|lt=Horayama Tumulus|Q11452574|WD=Q11452574|lt=Sugawara Fushimi Toryo}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}} tomb) and Sugawara Fushimi West Mausoleum ({{ill|lt=Emperor Anko|Q361010|WD=Q361010}} tomb).・It is known that the name of Sugawara-dera Temple (currently {{ill|lt=Kikoji|Q11419051|WD=Q11419051}}) is derived from the place name of this area, and the name of the Sugawara clan is also said to be derived from the name of this area {{Sfn|Sugawara clan (ancient clan)|2010}}. The rituals at Sugawara Shrine are thought to have originated when the Haji clan of Sugawara (later known as the Sugawara clan) worshiped their ancestral deity, but in later generations it became the birthplace of Michizane Sugawara. Some theories arose, and it is currently worshiped as a shrine of {{ill|lt=Tenjin faith|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725}} {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1982}}. === History === Founding date is unknown. As mentioned above, it is presumed that the Haji clan (later {{ill|lt=Sugawara clan|Q1771125|WD=Q1771125}}), whose home base was {{ill|lt=honkan|Q846706|WD=Q846706}}, worshiped their ancestral deity. Later, when Sugawara-dera Temple ({{ill|lt=Kikoji|Q11419051|WD=Q11419051}}) was built in the southwest, it became its guardian shrine, and it came to be called both Sugawara Shrine and Kikoji Tenmangu Shrine. {{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=In the Shinmeicho}}, ``Sugawara Shrine'' is listed in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}{{ill|lt=Soshita District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415}}, and it is listed as a Shikinai Shrine {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1982}}.According to the shrine's biography, the shrine was destroyed by military fire during the {{ill|lt=Bunki|Q1324885|WD=Q1324885}} years ({{ill|lt=1501|Q6131|WD=Q6131}} - {{ill|lt=1504|Q6148|WD=Q6148}}), and also during the {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001}} years ({{ill|lt=1688|Q7671|WD=Q7671}} - There was also a fire in {{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|WD=Q6824}}). {{ill|lt=Kanpo|Q1145798|WD=Q1145798}} years ({{ill|lt=1741|Q7573|WD=Q7573}} - {{ill|lt=1744|Q7583|WD=Q7583}}) in {{ill|lt=Kofukuji|Q1070863|WD=Q1070863}}{{ill||lt=Ichijoin|UNKNOWN}}It is said that it was rebuilt by order of the Imperial Prince Miya Makoto. After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, it became independent from Kikoji Temple due to the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}}. In {{ill|lt=Modern Shrine Ranking System|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, it was listed as ``Sugawara Shrine'' in {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}{{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi)|1982}}. In {{ill|lt=2002|Q1987|WD=Q1987}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}}14), it was renamed to "Sugawara Tenmangu Shrine." === Precincts === [[File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|main hall}}]] * Main shrine * Noritoden * Hall of worship * Shrine office * Storehouse * Fudezuka * Front gate * Outside the precincts ** Tenjinbori - Called the birth pond of {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}. === Setsusha === * Haruhiko Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Haruhiko Watanai|Q92665997|WD=Q92665997}}Okinami * Inari Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Toyoukebime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Himekami}} * Ichikishima Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishima Himejin}} File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), keidaisha-1.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), keidaisha-2.jpg|Shinuchisha === Festival === The festivals held annually at Sugawara Tenmangu Shrine are as follows. * Monthly festival (25th of every month) * Onda Festival (February 25th) * Nara Fude Festival (March equinox) * Birthday Festival, Shinto Ritual (June 25th) * Regular festival (the day before Sports Day in October) * Niinamesai (November 25th) === Cultural assets === === Nara City designated tangible cultural property === [[File:Tosō Tenjin (Nara National Museum).jpg|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Colored Tosō Tenjin Statue on Silk
(Nara City Designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Painting on silk of Tosho Tenjin statue (painting) - Made in {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}}. {{ill|lt=Nara National Museum|Q147312|WD=Q147312}}Deposited. Designated on March 6, 2007 [http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1258080429654/index.html Colored Tosei Tenjin statue on silk] (Nara City homepage). . === Related locations === [[File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), Sugahara-in-ato.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Sugawara Tenmangu Ruins Tenjinbori}}]] * Sugawara Tenmangu Ruins Tenjinbori ({{Coord|34|41|8.16|N|135|46|47.10|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Sugawara Tenmangu Ruins Tenjinbori}}) : The site of the Sugawara clan's old mansion. In later generations, a theory arose that it was the birthplace of Michizane Sugawara, and the pond is believed to be the birthplace of Michizane. {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1982}}. === {{wikidata link|Q11619171}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Sugawara Tenmangu Shrine |Image = [[File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), haiden.jpg|280px]]
House of worship |Location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Nara City|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}Sugawara Higashi 1-15-1 |ISO = JP-29 |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 41|Latitude seconds = 8.76 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 46|Longitude seconds = 42.13 |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Amenohohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Amaho Nichi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Nomi-Sukune-no-Mikoto|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi-Sukune-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}Ko |Divinity = |Company rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchisha|UNKNOWN}} (small)
Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = {{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = October {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sports Day|UNKNOWN}} day before |Shinto ritual = |Label position = top |Map = Nara city }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), entrance.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]]'''Sugawara Tenmangu'' (Sugawara Tenmangu) is located in {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Nara City|Q169134|WD=Q169134}} Sugawara Higashi 1-chome.{{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}}. In {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. Also known as ``Sugawara Shrine''. It is known as a shrine related to the ancient clans {{ill|lt=Hashi clan|Q10927758|WD=Q10927758}} and {{ill|lt=Sugawara clan|Q1771125|WD=Q1771125}}. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are enshrined. . * {{ill|lt=Amenohohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Amenohohi no Mikoto}} (Amenohohi no Mikoto) * {{ill|lt=Nominosukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nominosukune no Mikoto}} (Nominosukune no Mikoto) * {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} (SugaharaMichizaneko) {{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} 5 years ({{ill|lt=927 years|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) “{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki|UNKNOWN}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho}}, the enshrined deity is listed as 1 {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1982}}. According to the shrine's legend, there was originally one enshrined deity, Tenho Nichimiko, but later Nomi no Sukune and Sugawara Michizane were enshrined, making the number three. === About the enshrined deity ===The main enshrined deity, {{ill|lt=Amenohohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Amenohohi}}, is described in ``{{ill|lt=Nihonshoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}'' as ``Kore Izumo. As you can see, he became the ancestor god of the ancient clan '''{{ill|lt=Haji clan|Q10927758|WD=Q10927758}}'' (Hajiuji, Hajiren and later Haji Sukune). The Haji clan is the {{ill|lt=Tomozo|Q11381545|WD=Q11381545}} clan of Hajibu, which is responsible for the production of {{ill|lt=haniwa|Q849376|WD=Q849376}}, {{ill|lt=earthenware|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111}}, funerals, and {{ill|lt=mausoleum|Q65292090|WD=Q65292090}} {{Sfn| The Haji clan (ancient clan) |2010}}, the distant ancestor of the Haji clan, {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742}}, is also the person whose Haniwa tradition is recorded in the Nihon Shoki. This Haji clan has four limbs, which appear as "four bells," in an article dated December 30, 9th year of {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} ({{ill|lt=790 years|Q31147|WD=Q31147}}) of "{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}". These are {{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q1047117|WD=Q1047117}} Mozu, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} Sugawara, Yamato Province Akishino, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}}{{ jalink|Shiki District|Q11491125}}, {{ill|lt=Tanbi District|Q11368538|WD=Q11368538}} are huge tumulus groups ({{ill|lt=Mozu Tumulus Group|Q4250584|WD=Q4250584}}, {{jalink|Saki Dunretsu Kofun {{Sfn|Hashi clan (ancient clan)|2010}}{{Sfn|Hashi (ancient clan encyclopedia)|2015}}, which corresponds to the site of the burial mound group|Q11383489|Saki kofun group}}/{{ill|lt=Furuichi kofun group|Q4250076|WD=Q4250076}}). Of the four tribes, the Mozu, Sugawara, and Akishino tribes were formed around the end of {{ill|lt=8th century|Q8086|WD=Q8086}}, respectively by the Oeda clan (later {{ill|lt=Oe clan|Q11436863|WD=Q11436863}}) and {{ill|lt=Oe clan|Q11436863|WD=Q11436863}}. |Sugawara clan|Q1771125}} and {{ill|lt=Akishino clan|Q65277285|WD=Q65277285}}.The place name ``Sugawara'' means the grassland and natural habitat of {{ill|lt=Sedge genus|Q158501|WD=Q158501|lt=Sedge}} (sedge), and the name originally referred to the vast hilly area west of {{ill|lt=Heijo Palace|Q211585|WD=Q211585}} {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shinai Shrine)|1982}}. Regarding the Haji clan branch clan in the Sugawara area, the ``Suga family biographies'' also states that the Haji clan resides as ``Since then, the Dobe clan has lived in Sugawara Fushimi Village'' {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In fact, the existence of the Haniwa kiln ruins of the late Sugawara Higashi Ruins (designated historical site by Nara City) from the late {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939}} area is known to support the existence of the Haji clan. Sugawara Higashi Ruins Haniwa Kiln Ruins] (Nara City homepage). It is also noteworthy that the area of ​​worship of Sugawara Shrine almost coincides with the distribution range of the Haji clan. In addition to Sugawara Shrine, history books also include {{ill|lt=Horayama Tumulus|Q11452574|WD=Q11452574|lt=Sugawara Fushimi Toryo}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}} tomb) and Sugawara Fushimi West Mausoleum ({{ill|lt=Emperor Anko|Q361010|WD=Q361010}} tomb).・It is known that the name of Sugawara-dera Temple (currently {{ill|lt=Kikoji|Q11419051|WD=Q11419051}}) is derived from the place name of this area, and the name of the Sugawara clan is also said to be derived from the name of this area {{Sfn|Sugawara clan (ancient clan)|2010}}. The rituals at Sugawara Shrine are thought to have originated when the Haji clan of Sugawara (later known as the Sugawara clan) worshiped their ancestral deity, but in later generations it became the birthplace of Michizane Sugawara. Some theories arose, and it is currently worshiped as a shrine of {{ill|lt=Tenjin faith|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725}} {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1982}}. === History === Founding date is unknown. As mentioned above, it is presumed that the Haji clan (later {{ill|lt=Sugawara clan|Q1771125|WD=Q1771125}}), whose home base was {{ill|lt=honkan|Q846706|WD=Q846706}}, worshiped their ancestral deity. Later, when Sugawara-dera Temple ({{ill|lt=Kikoji|Q11419051|WD=Q11419051}}) was built in the southwest, it became its guardian shrine, and it came to be called both Sugawara Shrine and Kikoji Tenmangu Shrine. {{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=In the Shinmeicho}}, ``Sugawara Shrine'' is listed in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}{{ill|lt=Soshita District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415}}, and it is listed as a Shikinai Shrine {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1982}}.According to the shrine's biography, the shrine was destroyed by military fire during the {{ill|lt=Bunki|Q1324885|WD=Q1324885}} years ({{ill|lt=1501|Q6131|WD=Q6131}} - {{ill|lt=1504|Q6148|WD=Q6148}}), and also during the {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001}} years ({{ill|lt=1688|Q7671|WD=Q7671}} - There was also a fire in {{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|WD=Q6824}}). {{ill|lt=Kanpo|Q1145798|WD=Q1145798}} years ({{ill|lt=1741|Q7573|WD=Q7573}} - {{ill|lt=1744|Q7583|WD=Q7583}}) in {{ill|lt=Kofukuji|Q1070863|WD=Q1070863}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichijoin|UNKNOWN}}It is said that it was rebuilt by order of the Imperial Prince Miya Makoto. After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, it became independent from Kikoji Temple due to the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}}. In {{ill|lt=Modern Shrine Ranking System|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, it was listed as ``Sugawara Shrine'' in {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}{{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi)|1982}}. In {{ill|lt=2002|Q1987|WD=Q1987}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}}14), it was renamed to "Sugawara Tenmangu Shrine." === Precincts === [[File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|main hall}}]] * Main shrine * Noritoden * Hall of worship * Shrine office * Storehouse * Fudezuka * Front gate * Outside the precincts ** Tenjinbori - Called the birth pond of {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}. === Setsusha === * Haruhiko Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Haruhiko Watanai|Q92665997|WD=Q92665997}}Okinami * Inari Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Toyoukebime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Himekami}} * Ichikishima Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishima Himejin}} File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), keidaisha-1.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), keidaisha-2.jpg|Shinuchisha === Festival === The festivals held annually at Sugawara Tenmangu Shrine are as follows. * Monthly festival (25th of every month) * Onda Festival (February 25th) * Nara Fude Festival (March equinox) * Birthday Festival, Shinto Ritual (June 25th) * Regular festival (the day before Sports Day in October) * Niinamesai (November 25th) === Cultural assets === === Nara City designated tangible cultural property === [[File:Tosō Tenjin (Nara National Museum).jpg|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Colored Tosō Tenjin Statue on Silk
(Nara City Designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Painting on silk of Tosho Tenjin statue (painting) - Made in {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}}. {{ill|lt=Nara National Museum|Q147312|WD=Q147312}}Deposited. Designated on March 6, 2007 [http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1258080429654/index.html Colored Tosei Tenjin statue on silk] (Nara City homepage). . === Related locations === [[File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), Sugahara-in-ato.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Sugawara Tenmangu Ruins Tenjinbori}}]] * Sugawara Tenmangu Ruins Tenjinbori ({{Coord|34|41|8.16|N|135|46|47.10|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Sugawara Tenmangu Ruins Tenjinbori}}) : The site of the Sugawara clan's old mansion. In later generations, a theory arose that it was the birthplace of Michizane Sugawara, and the pond is believed to be the birthplace of Michizane. {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1982}}. === In {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. Also known as ``Sugawara Shrine''. It is known as a shrine related to the ancient clans {{ill|lt=Hashi clan|Q10927758|WD=Q10927758}} and {{ill|lt=Sugawara clan|Q1771125|WD=Q1771125}}. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are enshrined. . * {{ill|lt=Amenohohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Amenohohi no Mikoto}} (Amenohohi no Mikoto) * {{ill|lt=Nominosukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nominosukune no Mikoto}} (Nominosukune no Mikoto) * {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} (SugaharaMichizaneko) {{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} 5 years ({{ill|lt=927 years|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) “{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki|1=UNKNOWN}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho}}, the enshrined deity is listed as 1 {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1982}}. According to the shrine's legend, there was originally one enshrined deity, Tenho Nichimiko, but later Nomi no Sukune and Sugawara Michizane were enshrined, making the number three. === About the enshrined deity ===The main enshrined deity, {{ill|lt=Amenohohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Amenohohi}}, is described in ``{{ill|lt=Nihonshoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}'' as ``Kore Izumo. As you can see, he became the ancestor god of the ancient clan '''{{ill|lt=Haji clan|Q10927758|WD=Q10927758}}'' (Hajiuji, Hajiren and later Haji Sukune). The Haji clan is the {{ill|lt=Tomozo|Q11381545|WD=Q11381545}} clan of Hajibu, which is responsible for the production of {{ill|lt=haniwa|Q849376|WD=Q849376}}, {{ill|lt=earthenware|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111}}, funerals, and {{ill|lt=mausoleum|Q65292090|WD=Q65292090}} {{Sfn| The Haji clan (ancient clan) |2010}}, the distant ancestor of the Haji clan, {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742}}, is also the person whose Haniwa tradition is recorded in the Nihon Shoki. This Haji clan has four limbs, which appear as "four bells," in an article dated December 30, 9th year of {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} ({{ill|lt=790 years|Q31147|WD=Q31147}}) of "{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}". These are {{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q1047117|WD=Q1047117}} Mozu, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} Sugawara, Yamato Province Akishino, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}}{{ jalink|Shiki District|Q11491125}}, {{ill|lt=Tanbi District|Q11368538|WD=Q11368538}} are huge tumulus groups ({{ill|lt=Mozu Tumulus Group|Q4250584|WD=Q4250584}}, {{jalink|Saki Dunretsu Kofun {{Sfn|Hashi clan (ancient clan)|2010}}{{Sfn|Hashi (ancient clan encyclopedia)|2015}}, which corresponds to the site of the burial mound group|Q11383489|Saki kofun group}}/{{ill|lt=Furuichi kofun group|Q4250076|WD=Q4250076}}). Of the four tribes, the Mozu, Sugawara, and Akishino tribes were formed around the end of {{ill|lt=8th century|Q8086|WD=Q8086}}, respectively by the Oeda clan (later {{ill|lt=Oe clan|Q11436863|WD=Q11436863}}) and {{ill|lt=Oe clan|Q11436863|WD=Q11436863}}. |Sugawara clan|Q1771125}} and {{ill|lt=Akishino clan|Q65277285|WD=Q65277285}}.The place name ``Sugawara'' means the grassland and natural habitat of {{ill|lt=Sedge genus|Q158501|WD=Q158501|lt=Sedge}} (sedge), and the name originally referred to the vast hilly area west of {{ill|lt=Heijo Palace|Q211585|WD=Q211585}} {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shinai Shrine)|1982}}. Regarding the Haji clan branch clan in the Sugawara area, the ``Suga family biographies'' also states that the Haji clan resides as ``Since then, the Dobe clan has lived in Sugawara Fushimi Village'' {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In fact, the existence of the Haniwa kiln ruins of the late Sugawara Higashi Ruins (designated historical site by Nara City) from the late {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939}} area is known to support the existence of the Haji clan. Sugawara Higashi Ruins Haniwa Kiln Ruins] (Nara City homepage). It is also noteworthy that the area of ​​worship of Sugawara Shrine almost coincides with the distribution range of the Haji clan. In addition to Sugawara Shrine, history books also include {{ill|lt=Horayama Tumulus|Q11452574|WD=Q11452574|lt=Sugawara Fushimi Toryo}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}} tomb) and Sugawara Fushimi West Mausoleum ({{ill|lt=Emperor Anko|Q361010|WD=Q361010}} tomb).・It is known that the name of Sugawara-dera Temple (currently {{ill|lt=Kikoji|Q11419051|WD=Q11419051}}) is derived from the place name of this area, and the name of the Sugawara clan is also said to be derived from the name of this area {{Sfn|Sugawara clan (ancient clan)|2010}}. The rituals at Sugawara Shrine are thought to have originated when the Haji clan of Sugawara (later known as the Sugawara clan) worshiped their ancestral deity, but in later generations it became the birthplace of Michizane Sugawara. Some theories arose, and it is currently worshiped as a shrine of {{ill|lt=Tenjin faith|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725}} {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1982}}. === History === Founding date is unknown. As mentioned above, it is presumed that the Haji clan (later {{ill|lt=Sugawara clan|Q1771125|WD=Q1771125}}), whose home base was {{ill|lt=honkan|Q846706|WD=Q846706}}, worshiped their ancestral deity. Later, when Sugawara-dera Temple ({{ill|lt=Kikoji|Q11419051|WD=Q11419051}}) was built in the southwest, it became its guardian shrine, and it came to be called both Sugawara Shrine and Kikoji Tenmangu Shrine. {{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=In the Shinmeicho}}, ``Sugawara Shrine'' is listed in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}{{ill|lt=Soshita District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415}}, and it is listed as a Shikinai Shrine {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1982}}.According to the shrine's biography, the shrine was destroyed by military fire during the {{ill|lt=Bunki|Q1324885|WD=Q1324885}} years ({{ill|lt=1501|Q6131|WD=Q6131}} - {{ill|lt=1504|Q6148|WD=Q6148}}), and also during the {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001}} years ({{ill|lt=1688|Q7671|WD=Q7671}} - There was also a fire in {{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|WD=Q6824}}). {{ill|lt=Kanpo|Q1145798|WD=Q1145798}} years ({{ill|lt=1741|Q7573|WD=Q7573}} - {{ill|lt=1744|Q7583|WD=Q7583}}) in {{ill|lt=Kofukuji|Q1070863|WD=Q1070863}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichijoin|1=UNKNOWN}}It is said that it was rebuilt by order of the Imperial Prince Miya Makoto. After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, it became independent from Kikoji Temple due to the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}}. In {{ill|lt=Modern Shrine Ranking System|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, it was listed as ``Sugawara Shrine'' in {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}{{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi)|1982}}. In {{ill|lt=2002|Q1987|WD=Q1987}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}}14), it was renamed to "Sugawara Tenmangu Shrine." === Precincts === [[File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|main hall}}]] * Main shrine * Noritoden * Hall of worship * Shrine office * Storehouse * Fudezuka * Front gate * Outside the precincts ** Tenjinbori - Called the birth pond of {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}. === Setsusha === * Haruhiko Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Haruhiko Watanai|Q92665997|WD=Q92665997}}Okinami * Inari Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Toyoukebime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Himekami}} * Ichikishima Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishima Himejin}} File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), keidaisha-1.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), keidaisha-2.jpg|Shinuchisha === Festival === The festivals held annually at Sugawara Tenmangu Shrine are as follows. * Monthly festival (25th of every month) * Onda Festival (February 25th) * Nara Fude Festival (March equinox) * Birthday Festival, Shinto Ritual (June 25th) * Regular festival (the day before Sports Day in October) * Niinamesai (November 25th) === Cultural assets === === Nara City designated tangible cultural property === [[File:Tosō Tenjin (Nara National Museum).jpg|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Colored Tosō Tenjin Statue on Silk
(Nara City Designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Painting on silk of Tosho Tenjin statue (painting) - Made in {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}}. {{ill|lt=Nara National Museum|Q147312|WD=Q147312}}Deposited. Designated on March 6, 2007 [http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1258080429654/index.html Colored Tosei Tenjin statue on silk] (Nara City homepage). . === Related locations === [[File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), Sugahara-in-ato.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Sugawara Tenmangu Ruins Tenjinbori}}]] * Sugawara Tenmangu Ruins Tenjinbori ({{Coord|34|41|8.16|N|135|46|47.10|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Sugawara Tenmangu Ruins Tenjinbori}}) : The site of the Sugawara clan's old mansion. In later generations, a theory arose that it was the birthplace of Michizane Sugawara, and the pond is believed to be the birthplace of Michizane. {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1982}}. === {{wikidata link|Q11619171}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Sugawara Tenmangu Shrine |Image = [[File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), haiden.jpg|280px]]
House of worship |Location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Nara City|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}Sugawara Higashi 1-15-1 |ISO = JP-29 |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 41|Latitude seconds = 8.76 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 46|Longitude seconds = 42.13 |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Amenohohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Amaho Nichi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Nomi-Sukune-no-Mikoto|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi-Sukune-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}Ko |Divinity = |Company rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = {{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = October {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}} day before |Shinto ritual = |Label position = top |Map = Nara city }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), entrance.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]]'''Sugawara Tenmangu'' (Sugawara Tenmangu) is located in {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Nara City|Q169134|WD=Q169134}} Sugawara Higashi 1-chome.{{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}}. In {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. Also known as ``Sugawara Shrine''. It is known as a shrine related to the ancient clans {{ill|lt=Hashi clan|Q10927758|WD=Q10927758}} and {{ill|lt=Sugawara clan|Q1771125|WD=Q1771125}}. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are enshrined. . * {{ill|lt=Amenohohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Amenohohi no Mikoto}} (Amenohohi no Mikoto) * {{ill|lt=Nominosukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nominosukune no Mikoto}} (Nominosukune no Mikoto) * {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} (SugaharaMichizaneko) {{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} 5 years ({{ill|lt=927 years|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) “{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki|1=UNKNOWN}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho}}, the enshrined deity is listed as 1 {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1982}}. According to the shrine's legend, there was originally one enshrined deity, Tenho Nichimiko, but later Nomi no Sukune and Sugawara Michizane were enshrined, making the number three. === About the enshrined deity ===The main enshrined deity, {{ill|lt=Amenohohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Amenohohi}}, is described in ``{{ill|lt=Nihonshoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}'' as ``Kore Izumo. As you can see, he became the ancestor god of the ancient clan '''{{ill|lt=Haji clan|Q10927758|WD=Q10927758}}'' (Hajiuji, Hajiren and later Haji Sukune). The Haji clan is the {{ill|lt=Tomozo|Q11381545|WD=Q11381545}} clan of Hajibu, which is responsible for the production of {{ill|lt=haniwa|Q849376|WD=Q849376}}, {{ill|lt=earthenware|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111}}, funerals, and {{ill|lt=mausoleum|Q65292090|WD=Q65292090}} {{Sfn| The Haji clan (ancient clan) |2010}}, the distant ancestor of the Haji clan, {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742}}, is also the person whose Haniwa tradition is recorded in the Nihon Shoki. This Haji clan has four limbs, which appear as "four bells," in an article dated December 30, 9th year of {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} ({{ill|lt=790 years|Q31147|WD=Q31147}}) of "{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}". These are {{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q1047117|WD=Q1047117}} Mozu, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} Sugawara, Yamato Province Akishino, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}}{{ jalink|Shiki District|Q11491125}}, {{ill|lt=Tanbi District|Q11368538|WD=Q11368538}} are huge tumulus groups ({{ill|lt=Mozu Tumulus Group|Q4250584|WD=Q4250584}}, {{jalink|Saki Dunretsu Kofun {{Sfn|Hashi clan (ancient clan)|2010}}{{Sfn|Hashi (ancient clan encyclopedia)|2015}}, which corresponds to the site of the burial mound group|Q11383489|Saki kofun group}}/{{ill|lt=Furuichi kofun group|Q4250076|WD=Q4250076}}). Of the four tribes, the Mozu, Sugawara, and Akishino tribes were formed around the end of {{ill|lt=8th century|Q8086|WD=Q8086}}, respectively by the Oeda clan (later {{ill|lt=Oe clan|Q11436863|WD=Q11436863}}) and {{ill|lt=Oe clan|Q11436863|WD=Q11436863}}. |Sugawara clan|Q1771125}} and {{ill|lt=Akishino clan|Q65277285|WD=Q65277285}}.The place name ``Sugawara'' means the grassland and natural habitat of {{ill|lt=Sedge genus|Q158501|WD=Q158501|lt=Sedge}} (sedge), and the name originally referred to the vast hilly area west of {{ill|lt=Heijo Palace|Q211585|WD=Q211585}} {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shinai Shrine)|1982}}. Regarding the Haji clan branch clan in the Sugawara area, the ``Suga family biographies'' also states that the Haji clan resides as ``Since then, the Dobe clan has lived in Sugawara Fushimi Village'' {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In fact, the existence of the Haniwa kiln ruins of the late Sugawara Higashi Ruins (designated historical site by Nara City) from the late {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939}} area is known to support the existence of the Haji clan. Sugawara Higashi Ruins Haniwa Kiln Ruins] (Nara City homepage). It is also noteworthy that the area of ​​worship of Sugawara Shrine almost coincides with the distribution range of the Haji clan. In addition to Sugawara Shrine, history books also include {{ill|lt=Horayama Tumulus|Q11452574|WD=Q11452574|lt=Sugawara Fushimi Toryo}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}} tomb) and Sugawara Fushimi West Mausoleum ({{ill|lt=Emperor Anko|Q361010|WD=Q361010}} tomb).・It is known that the name of Sugawara-dera Temple (currently {{ill|lt=Kikoji|Q11419051|WD=Q11419051}}) is derived from the place name of this area, and the name of the Sugawara clan is also said to be derived from the name of this area {{Sfn|Sugawara clan (ancient clan)|2010}}. The rituals at Sugawara Shrine are thought to have originated when the Haji clan of Sugawara (later known as the Sugawara clan) worshiped their ancestral deity, but in later generations it became the birthplace of Michizane Sugawara. Some theories arose, and it is currently worshiped as a shrine of {{ill|lt=Tenjin faith|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725}} {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1982}}. === History === Founding date is unknown. As mentioned above, it is presumed that the Haji clan (later {{ill|lt=Sugawara clan|Q1771125|WD=Q1771125}}), whose home base was {{ill|lt=honkan|Q846706|WD=Q846706}}, worshiped their ancestral deity. Later, when Sugawara-dera Temple ({{ill|lt=Kikoji|Q11419051|WD=Q11419051}}) was built in the southwest, it became its guardian shrine, and it came to be called both Sugawara Shrine and Kikoji Tenmangu Shrine. {{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=In the Shinmeicho}}, ``Sugawara Shrine'' is listed in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}{{ill|lt=Soshita District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415}}, and it is listed as a Shikinai Shrine {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1982}}.According to the shrine's biography, the shrine was destroyed by military fire during the {{ill|lt=Bunki|Q1324885|WD=Q1324885}} years ({{ill|lt=1501|Q6131|WD=Q6131}} - {{ill|lt=1504|Q6148|WD=Q6148}}), and also during the {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001}} years ({{ill|lt=1688|Q7671|WD=Q7671}} - There was also a fire in {{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|WD=Q6824}}). {{ill|lt=Kanpo|Q1145798|WD=Q1145798}} years ({{ill|lt=1741|Q7573|WD=Q7573}} - {{ill|lt=1744|Q7583|WD=Q7583}}) in {{ill|lt=Kofukuji|Q1070863|WD=Q1070863}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichijoin|1=UNKNOWN}}It is said that it was rebuilt by order of the Imperial Prince Miya Makoto. After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, it became independent from Kikoji Temple due to the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}}. In {{ill|lt=Modern Shrine Ranking System|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, it was listed as ``Sugawara Shrine'' in {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}{{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi)|1982}}. In {{ill|lt=2002|Q1987|WD=Q1987}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}}14), it was renamed to "Sugawara Tenmangu Shrine." === Precincts === [[File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|main hall}}]] * Main shrine * Noritoden * Hall of worship * Shrine office * Storehouse * Fudezuka * Front gate * Outside the precincts ** Tenjinbori - Called the birth pond of {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}. === Setsusha === * Haruhiko Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Haruhiko Watanai|Q92665997|WD=Q92665997}}Okinami * Inari Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Toyoukebime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Himekami}} * Ichikishima Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishima Himejin}} File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), keidaisha-1.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), keidaisha-2.jpg|Shinuchisha === Festival === The festivals held annually at Sugawara Tenmangu Shrine are as follows. * Monthly festival (25th of every month) * Onda Festival (February 25th) * Nara Fude Festival (March equinox) * Birthday Festival, Shinto Ritual (June 25th) * Regular festival (the day before Sports Day in October) * Niinamesai (November 25th) === Cultural assets === === Nara City designated tangible cultural property === [[File:Tosō Tenjin (Nara National Museum).jpg|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Colored Tosō Tenjin Statue on Silk
(Nara City Designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Painting on silk of Tosho Tenjin statue (painting) - Made in {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}}. {{ill|lt=Nara National Museum|Q147312|WD=Q147312}}Deposited. Designated on March 6, 2007 [http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1258080429654/index.html Colored Tosei Tenjin statue on silk] (Nara City homepage). . === Related locations === [[File:Sugahara-jinja (Nara), Sugahara-in-ato.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Sugawara Tenmangu Ruins Tenjinbori}}]] * Sugawara Tenmangu Ruins Tenjinbori ({{Coord|34|41|8.16|N|135|46|47.10|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Sugawara Tenmangu Ruins Tenjinbori}}) : The site of the Sugawara clan's old mansion. In later generations, a theory arose that it was the birthplace of Michizane Sugawara, and the pond is believed to be the birthplace of Michizane. {{Sfn|Sugawara Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1982}}. ===
